pattern: Since the number of remaining lexicographically-smaller completions from any partial position is just a combinations count, you can decide each next move -- H or V -- one character at a time instead of generating and sorting every path.
hints: At any point you're standing some number of rows and columns away from the destination -- and since 'H' sorts before 'V', first ask how many complete paths from here start with an H.
The number of completions that start with H is exactly the number of ways to arrange all the remaining moves after that H is taken -- a combinations count of the rows and columns still left to cover.

If k falls within that H-count, commit to H and shrink the remaining columns by one; otherwise subtract that count from k, commit to V, and shrink the remaining rows by one -- repeat the same decision until no moves are left.

edge_inputs.description: maximum destination, lexicographically last path (max valid k)
k at the maximum valid value for a mid-size destination (tests the H/V count boundary exactly)

stress.description: random destinations across the full range with k drawn from the valid range for that destination
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    from math import comb
    row = rng.randint(1, 15)
    col = rng.randint(1, 15)
    total = comb(row + col, row)
    k = rng.randint(1, total)
    return [[row, col], k]
